name: Cuerno la Mora and Carrasconte Sanctuary Loop
name_original: Laguna de la Miranda y Santuario de Carrasconte por el canal – circular desde Mena de Babia por la Reserva de la Biosfera del Valle de Laciana
description: This difficult 20.5-mile mountain bike route explores the Laciana Valley Biosphere Reserve, featuring Laguna de la Miranda and Santuario de
region_intro: Mountain biking around Cabrillanes, located in the province of León, offers diverse terrain within the Cantabrian Mountains and the Babia Biosphere Reserve. The region is characterized by mountainous landscapes, expansive valleys, and vast pastures, with elevations ranging between 1,150 and 1,249 meters. This environment provides a variety of natural features, including glacial lakes and significant forests, suitable for mountain bike trails. Best mountain bike trails around Cabrillanes The most popular mountain bike trail is La Farrapona and Somiedo Lakes Loop, a 31.7 miles (51.1 km) trail that takes 6 hours 0 minutes to complete. This difficult route navigates high mountain passes and past glacial lakes. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Laguna de las Verdes, Laguna la Recoleta y Valle de Valverde – circular desde San Emiliano por la Reserva de la biosfera de Babia, a difficult 29.0 miles (46.7 km) path. This demanding trail explores the Babia Biosphere Reserve and its notable lakes. Local mountain bikers also love the Cuerno la Mora and Carrasconte Sanctuary Loop, a 20.5 miles (32.9 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain, often completed in about 3 hours 7 minutes. Mountain biking around Cabrillanes is defined by mountainous landscapes, expansive valleys, and glacial lakes, with options ranging from easy to difficult routes. The network offers options for different ability levels, including 1 easy, 12 moderate, and 30 difficult routes. The routes in Cabrillanes are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 300 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Cabrillanes's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Cabrillanes?
A: There are over 40 mountain bike trails around Cabrillanes listed on komoot, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Cabrillanes?
A: The trails in Cabrillanes, situated within the Cantabrian Mountains and the Babia Biosphere Reserve, feature diverse terrain. You can expect mountainous landscapes, expansive valleys, vast pastures, and glacial lakes. The region's elevation ranges between 1,150 and 1,249 meters, providing varied natural features.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Cabrillanes?
A: Yes, while many routes are challenging, there is at least one easy mountain bike trail available. For those looking for a moderate challenge, there are 12 moderate routes to explore.

Q: Are there challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders in Cabrillanes?
A: Absolutely. Cabrillanes is known for its demanding terrain, with 30 difficult mountain bike routes available. Trails like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1037482035/la-farrapona-and-somiedo-lakes-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Farrapona and Somiedo Lakes Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a 51.1 km trail navigating high mountain passes and glacial lakes, offer significant challenges.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while mountain biking in Cabrillanes?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can encounter stunning glacial lakes such as Laguna de Lago de Babia and Laguna de las Verdes. The Babia Biosphere Reserve itself is a highlight, with areas like the Valle de San Emiliano and the ancient Monte Carcedo Pine Forest. You might also pass by traditional structures like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6136136" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Teito of Braña La Corba&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5473402" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Refugio Laguna Las Verdes&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Cabrillanes?
A: Yes, many routes in Cabrillanes are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1037632822/cuerno-la-mora-and-carrasconte-sanctuary-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cuerno la Mora and Carrasconte Sanctuary Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 32.9 km long and takes about 3 hours and 7 minutes to complete.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Cabrillanes?
A: The mountain bike routes in Cabrillanes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 300 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the stunning mountainous landscapes and diverse natural features.

Q: Are there family-friendly mountain bike trails in Cabrillanes?
A: While many trails are rated moderate to difficult, there is at least one easy route that could be suitable for families. It's always recommended to check the specific trail details, including distance and elevation, to ensure it matches your family's skill level.

Q: Is parking available for mountain bikers in Cabrillanes?
A: Specific parking information varies by trailhead. However, as a municipality focused on outdoor activities, you will generally find parking options in and around the villages that serve as starting points for trails. It's advisable to check the route details on komoot for specific parking recommendations for each tour.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Cabrillanes?
A: Given its mountainous location and potential for snow at higher elevations, the best time for mountain biking in Cabrillanes is typically from late spring through early autumn (May to October). This period generally offers more favorable weather conditions and accessible trails.

Q: Are mountain bike trails in Cabrillanes dog-friendly?
A: Many trails in natural areas like Cabrillanes are dog-friendly, especially if your dog is accustomed to long distances and varied terrain. However, it's always best to keep dogs on a leash, particularly in areas with livestock or protected wildlife within the Babia Biosphere Reserve. Check individual route descriptions for any specific restrictions.

Q: What accommodation options are available near the mountain bike trails in Cabrillanes?
A: Cabrillanes and the surrounding Babia region offer various accommodation options, including rural guesthouses, small hotels, and apartments. These are typically found in the villages, providing convenient access to the trailheads and local amenities.

faqs.question: How challenging is this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as a difficult mountain bike ride. It requires a good level of physical fitness and advanced riding skills. Be prepared for technically demanding sections, where you might even need to push your bike.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 32.9 kilometers (20.4 miles) long with about 600 meters (1,968 feet) of elevation gain and descent. It is estimated to take mountain bikers around 3 hours and 7 minutes to complete.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain combines forest tracks with singletrack sections, presenting considerable technical and physical challenges. The route also features a distinctive 'canal' segment, which might offer different riding conditions or views as it follows a waterway.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: Key points of interest include the scenic Laguna de la Miranda and the historic Santuario de Carrasconte. You'll also pass through areas like Vega de la Cogulla, and ride along the Path to the Sanctuary of Nuestra Señora de Carrasconte and a Path Near Quintanilla de Babia.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: Given its demanding nature and mountain setting, the best time to ride is typically during spring or autumn. These seasons offer more moderate temperatures and beautiful scenery, especially with the lush beech and birch forests. Avoid extreme winter conditions or very hot summer days.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail in Mena de Babia?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends circularly from Mena de Babia. While specific parking details are not provided, you should look for designated parking areas within or near the village that accommodate trail users. It's advisable to arrive early, especially on busy days.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The trail is located within the Reserva de la Biosfera del Valle de Laciana, a vital habitat for wildlife. While dogs are generally permitted on trails, it is crucial to keep them on a leash at all times to protect local wildlife, especially species like the brown bear and capercaillie, and to respect other trail users.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to ride this trail or enter the Biosphere Reserve?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for mountain biking on public trails within the Reserva de la Biosfera del Valle de Laciana. However, always respect local regulations, stay on marked paths, and practice Leave No Trace principles to preserve the natural environment.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with any other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with other official trails. You will encounter parts of the Ruta de las Cuencas Mineras del Carbón, Bike Mirena Laciana, Camino Natural Via Verde de Villablino a Vilaseca, and the Ferrocarril Ponferrada - Villablino.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife might I encounter in the Laciana Valley Biosphere Reserve?
faqs.answer: The Reserva de la Biosfera del Valle de Laciana is known for its rich biodiversity. You might have the chance to spot iconic species such as the brown bear and capercaillie, among other local fauna. Always maintain a respectful distance and do not disturb wildlife.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this mountain bike ride?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's difficulty and length, it's essential to bring plenty of water, snacks, a repair kit for your bike, appropriate safety gear (helmet, gloves), and navigation tools. Weather can change quickly in mountain environments, so pack layers of clothing.
